name: Misrata Power Station
notes: 650 MW open-cycle plant with two F-class gas turbines. One of the newer additions to the GECOL fleet.

name: Tripoli West Power Station
notes: 690 MW open-cycle plant with four E-class gas turbines. Major supply for greater Tripoli region. Also known as West Mountain station.

name: Zliten Power Station
notes: 164 MW plant with four 41 MW gas turbines. Located on the coast between Tripoli and Misrata.

name: Ubari Power Station
notes: Unit 4 converted from crude oil to natural gas. Key power source for the Fezzan region. Previously ran on crude oil due to gas supply constraints.

name: South Tripoli Power Station
notes: 500 MW open-cycle gas turbine plant serving greater Tripoli. One of the larger capacity stations in the western network.

name: North Benghazi Power Station
notes: 740 MW plant serving eastern Libya's largest city. Critical infrastructure for Benghazi and surrounding area.

name: Zueitina Power Station
notes: 390 MW plant near Zueitina oil export terminal. Supplies power to the Gulf of Sirte oil & gas infrastructure.

name: Al-Sarir Power Station
notes: 200 MW plant in the Sarir oil field region. Supplies power to oil production facilities and the Great Man-Made River project infrastructure.

name: Derna Power Station
notes: One of Libya's older coastal stations. Original steam units from 1983, gas turbines added later. Sustained damage during conflicts.

name: Sabha Power Station
notes: 350 MW plant serving the Fezzan capital. Primary power supply for southern Libya's largest population center.

name: Al Khoms Power Station
notes: 620 MW combined steam and gas turbine station. One of the few stations with steam cycle capability. Located near the ancient city of Leptis Magna.

name: Sirte Power Station
notes: 350 MW plant on the Gulf of Sirte coast. Serves central coastal region. Some units damaged during 2011 conflict.

name: Zawiya Power Station
notes: 660 MW plant near Zawiya oil refinery. Serves the western coastal corridor and Zawiya industrial zone.

name: Tobruk Power Station
notes: 460 MW plant serving easternmost Libya. Key supply for Tobruk and the Cyrenaica eastern region. Can operate on diesel backup.
